_doc: Phase T1.1 (2026-07-11) — publish-time security review patterns for EndProductSecurityAgent.staticAnalysis. Extracted from the former hardcoded Swift array (RULE #1: pattern lists are content, not skeleton). The binary keeps a frozen _fallback_only copy for offline first-launch; this file is the source of truth and hot-loads via MchataisourceCacheService. blockSeverities lists the severity tiers that BLOCK publish (typed error before any bytes are uploaded); everything else remains advisory (securityFlags + moderationStatus). Fix vs the old Swift list: the credential-access regex used escaped pipes (matched a literal 'password|token|secret|key' string, so it never fired) — corrected to real alternation here.

patterns.description: Use of eval() can execute arbitrary code
Dynamic Function constructor can execute arbitrary code
Accessing document.cookie could leak session data
Opening new windows could redirect users
Using Web Crypto API — verify purpose
Loading external scripts is a security risk
postMessage could send data to parent frames
